claimText: The World Network of Biosphere Reserves (WNBR) is managed by UNESCO's Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme, which was launched in 1971.
evidenceExcerpt: WNBR (launched 1976) is managed by UNESCO's Man and the Biosphere (MAB) Programme (launched 1971).
examUse: Statement-based question on UNESCO MAB and WNBR origins.
distractorHooks: Managed by UNEP or IUCN instead of UNESCO
MAB launched in 1992 (Earth Summit) instead of 1971

claimText: Designations to the WNBR are made by the Director-General of UNESCO following decisions by the MAB International Coordinating Council (ICC) using the Seville Strategy.
evidenceExcerpt: Designations are made by the Director-General of UNESCO following MAB ICC decisions, using the Seville Strategy.
examUse: Statement-based question on the procedure and strategy used for WNBR designation.
distractorHooks: Designations made by the UN General Assembly
Uses the Madrid Action Plan or Aichi Targets instead of the Seville Strategy

claimText: India has 13 Biosphere Reserves recognized under UNESCO's World Network of Biosphere Reserves (WNBR).
evidenceExcerpt: India has 13 Biosphere Reserves recognized under WNBR.
examUse: Fact-based statement in a multi-statement question on India's Biosphere Reserves.
distractorHooks: India has 18 BRs recognized under WNBR
India has 14 BRs recognized under WNBR

claimText: The Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve in Himachal Pradesh encompasses Pin Valley National Park, Chandratal, and the Sarchu and Kibber Wildlife Sanctuaries.
evidenceExcerpt: Cold Desert BR (Himachal Pradesh) covers Pin Valley NP, Chandratal, and Sarchu & Kibber WLS.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question mapping protected areas to their respective Biosphere Reserves.
distractorHooks: Located in Ladakh or Uttarakhand instead of Himachal Pradesh
Includes Hemis National Park instead of Pin Valley NP

claimText: The Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve is located in a rain-shadow zone and is characterized by a distinct Buddhist culture and fauna such as the Snow Leopard and Himalayan Ibex.
evidenceExcerpt: Cold Desert BR lies in a rain-shadow zone with distinct Buddhist culture (Gompas, Chorten) and fauna like Snow Leopard and Himalayan Ibex.
examUse: Feature-based identification question describing a specific Biosphere Reserve.
distractorHooks: Fauna includes the Red Panda or Clouded Leopard
